title: Trailian

The '''Trailians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of great beauty. Native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Trailia]], they had developed a low technology society by the time of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. During that period, [[Cronal]], one of the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]], established a [[Cronal's laboratory|laboratory]] on the planet and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|experimented]] on Trailian subjects, turning the once peaceful and docile individuals into ferocious [[Trailian sithspawn|warriors]]. Those creations were unleashed on nearby worlds before being [[Battle of Trailia|defeated]] by a group of offworlders.

==Biology and appearance==
The Trailians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. They were all [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] with a great deal of raw potential and had great physical beauty.<ref name="SWGS" />

==Society and culture==
The Trailians had a low technology society. They lived in villages, dwelling in harmony with their [[Planet/Legends|world's]] environment. What little technology they did have consisted of adaptations of what already occurred in nature. Culturally, the Trailians were a peaceful people; however, once experimented upon, they became powerful warriors with devastating combat and force abilities.<ref name="SWGS" />

==History==
[[File:Cronal_GTCG-AoD.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Cronal twisted his Trailian subjects into ferocious warriors.]]
The Trailians evolved on [[Trailia]], a lush tropical planet in the distant<ref name="SWGS" /> [[Dominus sector/Legends|Dominus sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], near the [[Dark Nebula/Legends|Dark Nebula]].<ref name="Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> They had developed a peaceful, low technology society by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. However, that existence was disturbed when [[Cronal]], one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] most powerful [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] servants<ref name="SWGS" /> and an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]],<ref name="Pawns">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> discovered the planet sometime after his exile. He established a [[Cronal's laboratory|hidden laboratory]] deep within the planet's jungles and began to capture native Trailians.<ref name="SWGS" />

Using a combination of genetic engineering and dark side [[Sith alchemy/Legends|experimentation]], Cronal twisted his captives into a savage race of [[Trailian sithspawn]], hoping to use them to crush the New Republic. While some of those warriors were kept to guard his fortress where an army of them was being prepared alongside animals he had similarly twisted, Cronal dispatched warriors on missions to other worlds to test their combat skills. Word of unprovoked raids by those warriors on unsuspecting planets soon spread, and the remaining peaceful Trailians grew concerned about the increasing disappearances of their people. They offered a reward to anyone who would investigate, which attracted a group of offworlders to Trailia.<ref name="SWGS" />

{{RPG}}
The offworlders included a Force-sensitive individual who was able to detect the dark side emanating from Cronal's fortress and lead the group to it. In a climactic [[Battle of Trailia|battle]], they defeated the twisted Trailian guards and other beasts and routed the fortress. Cronal, however, escaped the battle. Similarly, some of his warriors found transport offworld and continued razing offworld cities until the same group of offworlders tracked them down and defeated them.<ref name="SWGS" />
{{Endgame}}

As for Cronal, he fled to the bowels planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], accompanied by several of his finest [[Trailian technobeast]]s. These Trailians were later killed when Cronal tried to transform the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Irek Ismaren]] into a colossal [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] warrior, leading to the newly-modified Ismaren going on a violent rampage.<ref name="warlords">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-3|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 3|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|On a lush tropical paradise, an evil Jedi master uses his hellish powers to bring about the destruction of a peaceful species and the creation of an evil army.|Bill Olmesdahl|<ref name="SWGS" />}}
The Trailians were created by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] for the ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'', a [[West End Games]] product for the second edition of their [[Roleplaying game|role-playing game]]. Published in [[1992]], the book contains adventure hooks to be fleshed out by game masters. The Trailian adventure is titled "[[The Monster Maker]]." In addition to the main proposed storyline, Olmesdahl offers an extension in which twisted Trailians make it offworld and the player characters encounter them on other worlds. This has been incorporated into the above storyline as an extension to the main campaign.

No date is provided for Cronal's experiments on the planet; however, it is during the administration of the New Republic and therefore after its founding in 5 ABY. In later sources, Cronal has been identified as both Blackhole<ref name="Pawns" /> and Lord Shadowspawn.<ref name="Mindor">''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]''</ref> As he is still alive up until at least [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="The Dark Forces Saga">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}}</ref> this adventure could occur anytime during the [[New Republic era]].
